---
title: "Ingest 10x Genomics data"
description: "FASTQ or raw counts → MuData with Cell Ranger."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 10
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Ingestion takes the raw output of a 10x Genomics run and produces a per-sample
count matrix stored as an `.h5mu` file, ready for
[processing and filtering](process-filter.qmd).

## Pick a starting point

The right workflow depends on what your sequencing provider delivered.

| Starting point | Library type | Workflow |
|---|---|---|
| BCL files | Any | `ingestion/demux` → then a mapping workflow |
| FASTQ | 10x GEX only | `ingestion/cellranger_mapping` |
| FASTQ | 10x multi (GEX + VDJ + ADT/HTO) | `ingestion/cellranger_multi` |
| 10x HDF5 (`.h5`) | Pre-generated | `convert/from_10xh5_to_h5mu` |
| 10x MEX (`.mtx`) | Pre-generated | `convert/from_10xmtx_to_h5mu` |

:::{.callout-tip}
If your provider already delivered a Cell Ranger count matrix, skip mapping and
use one of the converters directly.
:::

## Build a reference genome

Mapping needs a reference genome index. Build one with `make_reference`, or use
a pre-built 10x Genomics reference directly.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/make_reference/main.nf \
  --id GRCh38 \
  --genome_fasta Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.dna.primary_assembly.fa \
  --annotation_gtf Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.111.gtf \
  --output reference_genome/ \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Demultiplex BCL files (if needed)

If you start from raw Illumina BCL output, convert it to per-sample FASTQ files
first:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/demux/main.nf \
  --id run_001 \
  --input /path/to/bcl_run/ \
  --sample_sheet SampleSheet.csv \
  --output demux_output/ \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Map single-library samples (GEX only)

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_mapping/main.nf \
  --id sample_A \
  --input "fastq/sample_A/" \
  --reference reference_genome/ \
  --output sample_A.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Map multi-library samples (GEX + ADT/HTO + VDJ)

For CITE-seq or experiments with multiple library types captured from the same
cells, Cell Ranger Multi aligns all libraries jointly. The `feature_reference.csv`
describes the antibody or guide-RNA sequences used.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_multi/main.nf \
  --id sample_A \
  --input "fastq/sample_A/" \
  --gex_reference reference_genome/ \
  --feature_reference feature_reference.csv \
  --output sample_A.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Ingest many samples at once

Pass a [`param_list`](../concepts/param-list.qmd) to map a whole cohort in one
run. Example `samples.csv`:

```
id,input,reference
sample_A,fastq/sample_A/,reference_genome/
sample_B,fastq/sample_B/,reference_genome/
sample_C,fastq/sample_C/,reference_genome/
```

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_mapping/main.nf \
  --param_list samples.csv \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## What the output contains

Each sample becomes one `.h5mu` file. Depending on the library type it holds one
or more modalities:

| Modality key | Content |
|---|---|
| `rna` | RNA count matrix (cells × genes) |
| `prot` | ADT count matrix — antibody-derived tags (CITE-seq) |
| `vdj_b` / `vdj_t` | Immune repertoire (B/T cell receptor sequences) |
| `atac` | Chromatin accessibility (ATAC-seq peaks) |
| `gdo` | Guide RNA / CRISPR perturbation data |

Each modality stores the raw counts in `.X`, cell metadata in `.obs`, and
feature metadata in `.var`. No filtering has been applied yet — the matrix
contains both real cells and empty droplets.

**Next:** [Process & filter samples →](process-filter.qmd) ·
[every ingestion argument →](../reference/index.qmd)
